diagrams.title: Water Pollution Sources Overview
diagrams.description: Shows point sources (industrial pipes, sewage outfalls) and non-point sources (agricultural runoff, urban stormwater) entering water bodies
diagrams.purpose: Understanding pollution entry points and differences between point and non-point sources

diagrams.title: Eutrophication Process
diagrams.description: Illustrates the sequence from nutrient input to dead zone formation: nutrient runoff → algae bloom → light blockage → plant death → bacterial decomposition → oxygen depletion → fish kill
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how nutrient pollution creates dead zones in water bodies

diagrams.title: Biomagnification in Food Chain
diagrams.description: Shows concentration increasing at each trophic level: water → plankton → small fish → large fish → birds/humans
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how persistent pollutants concentrate up the food chain

diagrams.title: Wastewater Treatment Process
diagrams.description: Three-stage treatment flow: Primary (screening/sedimentation) → Secondary (biological treatment) → Tertiary (disinfection/filtration)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding sequential treatment to remove different types of pollutants
